sections.title: 1. DMA Subsystem Overview
sections.summary: High-level architecture of the DMA subsystem including block diagram, address map, and operating modes.

sections.title: 2. Descriptor Format
sections.summary: Defines the normal descriptor layout: source address, destination address, transfer size, control flags.

sections.title: 3. Linked-List Descriptor Mode
sections.summary: Linked-list mode allows chaining multiple descriptors via a next-pointer field. Enabled by DMA_CFG.LL_MODE_EN. Parser transitions to LINK_DESC state when ll_mode_en is asserted.

sections.title: 4. Scatter-Gather Descriptor Mode
sections.summary: Scatter-gather mode supports non-contiguous memory regions. Enabled by DMA_CFG.SG_MODE_EN.

sections.title: 5. Descriptor Chaining
sections.summary: Descriptor chaining allows back-to-back descriptor processing without CPU intervention. Chain of 3+ descriptors exercises the pipeline depth.

sections.title: 6. Address Alignment Rules
sections.summary: Source and destination addresses must be aligned to transfer size. Misaligned addresses trigger an error interrupt.

sections.title: 7. Interrupt System
sections.summary: DMA supports completion, error, and coalescing interrupts. Coalescing can be by count threshold or timeout. Masked interrupts remain pending until unmasked.

sections.title: 8. Error Handling
sections.summary: Error conditions: bus error, descriptor fetch error, alignment error, timeout. Each generates an error interrupt if unmasked.

sections.title: 9. Power Management
sections.summary: Clock gating on idle channels. Retention mode preserves register state during power-down. Retention mode is only available in specific silicon revisions.

sections.title: 10. AXI Burst Configuration
sections.summary: Supports INCR, WRAP, and FIXED burst types. Max burst length configurable up to 256 beats. WRAP burst wraps at power-of-2 boundaries.

sections.title: 11. Performance Characteristics
sections.summary: Back-to-back descriptor fetch achieves peak throughput when pipeline is full. Measured via monitor timestamps.
